chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.76 of 2026 (Tariq Khan vs. The State) Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz-ul-Hassan Shah For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 10.02.2026 Mr. Shahnawaz Brohi, advocate for applicant Mr. Mushraf Azhar, Special Prosecutor, ANF O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant, is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.47/2025, registered at Police Station ANF Clifton, u/s 6,7,9, 4, 14 & 15 CNS Act, 1997, by means of this application. 2. The Anti-Narcotics Force (ANF) arrested two co-accused on 30.09.2025, from Kolachi Hotel, Jamal Flyover, M9, Karachi, and from them allegedly 170 and 130 grams of ice respectively was recovered. The whole operation was conducted on the basis of spy information against the applicant whose name also transpired in the investigation. But he was not traced at the spot and subsequently was arrested from Peshawar and on his poination, from a flat in which he allegedly was residing, 1200 grams of charas and 300 grams of ice was recovered. 3. The case of the applicant for bail is that no recovery from him is shown in the FIR, and the alleged flat from which the recovery was effected is without any address; statement of the owner of said flat was recorded in which he has stated that he had rented the flat to someone else and not to the applicant, and co-accused have been granted bail. 4. On the other hand, Learned Special Prosecutor, ANF has opposed bail. 5. We have considered submissions of the parties and perused the material available on record. Prima facie, on the pointation of the applicant, a huge quantity of narcotics has been recovered; therefore, there are

We have considered submissions of the parties and perused the material available on record. Prima facie, on the pointation of the applicant, a huge quantity of narcotics has been recovered; therefore, there are 2 reasonable grounds to believe that applicant is involved in the offence of narcotics, which is considered an offence against society. The grounds raised in defence require deeper appreciation of evidence which cannot be undertaken at bail stage. No case for bail is made out. Accordingly, the bail application is dismissed. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA.
